stories.title: Norway joins France’s nuclear deterrence club
stories.summary: Norway is joining France’s plans to cooperate more closely with European countries on nuclear deterrence, the two countries’ leaders announced on Wednesday. The addition of Norway, which is traditionally very close to the United States, to the initiative is yet another example of how European countries are rethinking their security strategies in light of Russia’s war on Ukraine and concerns about the Trump administration’s reliability. “Along with nine other Europea

stories.title: To reverse the ‘greenlash’, Europe’s Green parties should embrace Polanski’s boldness | Tarik Abou-Chadi
stories.summary: Be more strident and ambitious, take on economic inequality, and progressive voters will reward you as they have the UK’s Greens Tarik Abou-Chadi is a professor of European politics at the University of Oxford European Green parties have been through a phase of stagnation and crisis in recent years. Long gone seem the days of the “green wave” across Europe. Back in 2019, Green parties secured their best-ever result in the European parliament elections, with 74 seats. In the same year, Green part

stories.title: Reuters executive Simon Robinson to replace Justin Stevens as ABC news director
stories.summary: Hugh Marks refuses to confirm or deny to Senate estimates that he threatened to terminate Stevens if he didn’t resign Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Sign up for Guardian Australia’s free weekly media newsletter here The ABC has confirmed a top news executive from Reuters, Simon Robinson, has been appointed as news director of the ABC, after Guardian Australia revealed the surprise appointee on Thursday morning. Robinson will join the ABC in September, four months after Ju

stories.title: Woman caught after 30 years on the run is jailed for armed robberies
stories.summary: Klette was finally caught in a flat in Berlin in 2024 and put on trial last year.

stories.title: Eljárás indult a stockholmi magyar nagykövetség egy munkatársa ellen, mert inzultusba keveredett magyar szomszédjával
stories.summary: Az affért videóra vette a sértett és felesége. A Külügyminisztérium kivizsgálja az ügyet.

stories.title: A 2010 utáni magyar növekedési modell: jól ment, csak nem messzire
stories.summary: Az adatok feketén-fehéren megmutatják, miért ütközött falakba a magyar gazdaság a 2020-as évekre: pont azokat a területeket nem támogatta a politika, amelyek a hosszú távú bővüléshez fontosak lennének.

stories.title: Fogy a magyar, kevesebb a foglalkoztatott
stories.summary: Egy év alatt 55 ezerrel csökkent a magyar foglalkoztatottak száma, pedig a munkanélküliség nem nőtt.

stories.title: A tolmács, aki kétszer nyert Bajnokok Ligáját
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére.

stories.title: Tabu vagy játék? Így tanulhatnak a gyerekek a pénzről
stories.summary: Szülőként sokan szeretnénk megtanítani a gyerekeinknek mindazt, amit nekünk senki sem magyarázott el. Csakhogy a pénzről beszélni otthon még ma is sokszor nehéz: nem mindig tudjuk, hogyan és mikor kezdjünk bele könnyedén, szorongás nélkül. Kíváncsiak voltunk, hogyan lehet közelebb vinni a gyerekekhez egy ilyen fontos témát, ezért elolvastuk A láthatatlan kincs című interaktív kalandregényt, ami játékos formában segít beszélgetni a pénzről, spórolásról és döntésekről már kisiskolás kortól.

stories.title: Így épült fel Magyarország pornóbirodalma – és most sorozat lett belőle
stories.summary: Van, amit a kilencvenes évek Magyarországán mindenki tudott, de senki sem mondott ki hangosan.

stories.title: Helyettes államtitkárként dolgozik majd a három kormányszóvivő
stories.summary: Magyar Péter május 28-i hatállyal nevezte ki Köböl Anitát, Magyar Évát és Szondi Vandát.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely: Meg kell védjem a miniszterelnököt, nem történt heves szóváltás
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a parlament keddi ülését megakasztva lépett oda Gulyás Gergelyhez, miután már nem kapott szót a házelnöktől. Gulyás szerint Magyar csak elmondta, hogy mi lett volna a válasza Gulyás felszólalására.

stories.title: Visszautasította a Fidesz a tiszás politikusok vádaskodását a diplomata-útlevelek ügyében
stories.summary: Azt állítja a korábbi kormánypárt, hogy szélesítette Magyarország nemzetközi kapcsolatrendszerét, ezért kellett sokaknak sokat utazni.

stories.title: Germany: Alleged leftist militant found guilty of robbery
stories.summary: Daniela Klette, suspected to be one of the last remaining members of the left-wing terrorist group Red Army Faction, has been found guilty for her part in a series of robberies — but more serious charges await.

stories.title: US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García
stories.summary: Judge said Trump administration would not have prosecuted Ábrego had he not challenged his high-profile deportation Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A US judge dismissed a criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García on Friday, finding that the Trump administration would not have prosecuted him had he not challenged his high-profile deportation. Ábrego, who had entered the United States without authorization in the past, became a symbol of the Trump administration’s drive fo

stories.title: Two killed in US strike on another alleged drug boat in Pacific as campaign’s death toll nears 200
stories.summary: Military claims boat targeted in second deadly attack this week was travelling along a known smuggling route The US military attacked what it called a drug trafficking boat on Wednesday in the eastern Pacific, killing two men, officials said. It was the second deadly attack in as many days in the Trump administration offensive that began last September and has now left almost 200 people dead. Rights groups say the attacks could amount to extrajudicial killings. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Gavan Reynolds cites ‘commercial-in-confidence nature of the contract’ in declining to answer Greens senator’s question in parliament Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The US private prison company running Australian immigration detention centres was slapped with penalties by the Australian government after allowing a dozen escapes, but the head of the Australian Border Force has declined to reveal the cost of the

stories.title: US launches airstrikes in Iran but says ceasefire still holds
stories.summary: The U.S. said Wednesday that it launched new airstrikes on Iran and took out four drones even as a broader ceasefire still holds in the nearly three-month old conflict. The airstrikes struck a military site along the southern coast of Iran that was deemed a threat to American forces and commercial maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, a U.S. official told POLITICO. The strikes were accompanied by U.S. forces downing four Iranian drones that also posed a threat, said the official, granted ano

stories.title: Trump cheers on Armenia’s prime minister ahead of election
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump endorsed Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan on Wednesday evening, calling him “a great friend and leader” ahead of the country’s parliamentary elections on June 7. In a post on Truth Social , Trump wrote: “Nikol completely shares my vision of PEACE and PROSPERITY for Armenia and the entire South Caucasus region … Nikol has my COMPLETE and TOTAL Endorsement for Re-Election on June 7, 2026.” Armenia is pivoting toward the West as frustration grows

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Eliminate Putin’s ‘last major advantage,’ Zelenskyy urges Washington
stories.summary: Patriots would neutralise ballistic missiles and force Russia to negotiate, says Ukrainian president; half a million Russian dead – GCHQ boss. What we know on day 1,555 Continue reading...

stories.title: US justice department reportedly opens criminal inquiry into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll
stories.summary: Officials said to be examining whether Carroll committed perjury in 2022 deposition tied to lawsuits against president The Trump administration has opened a criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll, the writer who accused the president of sexual assault, according to news reports . Prosecutors, the New York Times and CNN reported on Wednesday, are looking into whether Carroll, 82, committed perjury in a 2022 deposition during her civil lawsuits against Trump, in which she said she did not acce

stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. US justice department reportedly opens criminal inquiry into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll Sign up to the Breaking News US email President Trump is set to hold the 12th cabinet meeting of his second term at 11am EST on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Human rights lawyer Francesca Albanese on life under US sanctions - podcast
stories.summary: The UN’s special rapporteur for Palestine, Francesca Albanese, on the war in Gaza, living under US sanctions and accusations of antisemitism For a brief period this month, the human rights lawyer Francesca Albanese seemed to have some relief. In an extraordinary move in July last year, the Trump administration placed sanctions on the UN’s special rapporteur for Palestine – placing her alongside figures such as Vladimir Putin and Bashir al-Assad – over her engagement with the international crimin

stories.title: Brexit rules on food exports to be scrapped, government confirms
stories.summary: Agreement affecting meat, plants and packaging promises to end paperwork ‘hell’ and border delays from 2027 Business live – latest updates Brexit rules affecting UK food exports to the EU, including fresh sausages and burgers, will be scrapped from mid-2027 in the first confirmed result of Keir Starmer’s “reset” negotiations with Brussels, the government has announced. When the rules come into force, exporters of meat – whether fresh, frozen or processed – will no longer require costly veterinar

stories.title: Dormitory fire at Kenyan school kills at least 15 students
stories.summary: Parents face anxious wait for updates after blaze tears through Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county A fire that ripped through a dormitory at a girls’ school in Kenya overnight has killed at least 15 students, according to police, while dozens more were injured. Gilgil police station said at least 15 girls died at Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county, about 76 miles north-east of Nairobi, according to a police report seen by Reuters. Continue reading...

stories.title: Israel’s defence minister says large-scale Palestinian migration from Gaza will go ahead
stories.summary: Human rights groups and lawyers say policy amounts to ethnic cleansing Israel’s defence minister has said he is committed to the ethnic cleansing of Gaza through large-scale migration of Palestinians as part of Israel’s long-term plans for the territory. Israel Katz said the government would implement a plan for large numbers of Palestinians to leave Gaza “at the right time and in the right manner”, in a statement on Wednesday marking the targeted killing of Mohammed Odeh, Hamas’s most recent mi

Pat McFadden , the work and pensisons secretary, issued this statement overnight about the Milburn report into youth unemployment. stories.title: Concerns grow over conditions at ICE facility in New Jersey
stories.summary: Democratic members of Congress have toured the Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention center, which has become a focus point for opponents of US President Donald Trump's migration agenda.

stories.title: Blair screed uncorks fresh angst over what Labour wants to be, with or without Starmer
stories.summary: Former British Prime Minister Tony Blair intended his bracing meditation on the state of the nation to land as a shock to the center-left system and its inadequacy to face up to urgent problems for the U.K. at home and abroad. That aim has been fulfilled: His essay, in excess of 5,000 words, published late Tuesday, has dominated the political airwaves in the run-up to a crucial by-election in June and a potential summer leadership challenge to his Labour successor Keir Starme

stories.title: Pentagon puts building blocks in place for Cuba invasion
stories.summary: The Pentagon has spent months positioning the troops and weapons needed for the U.S. to launch a military attack on Cuba — all it needs is a final go-ahead from Donald Trump. The president has floated an invasion of the island after economic and political pressure failed to topple the Communist government. But the Navy’s built-up presence in the region — the largest in the world outside the Middle East — would allow the U.S. to act immediately. These strategically placed assets set the table for

stories.title: Spanish PM’s family affair: the corruption cases involving Pedro Sánchez’s brother, wife and predecessor
stories.summary: Socialist leader insists no wrongdoing by family and backs former prime minister, but has much to contend with Spain’s prime minister, Pedro Sánchez, is facing a long and difficult summer as corruption cases involving his brother, his wife and his predecessor José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero come before judges over the coming days and weeks. The socialist leader – who took power eight years ago after using a vote of no confidence to topple the corruption-mired government of the conservative People’s

stories.title: Trump threatens to ‘blow up’ US ally Oman amid talks over strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US president calls on US ally to ‘behave … or else we’ll have to blow them up’ in casual aside during cabinet meeting Donald Trump has threatened to “blow up” Oman if it fails to “behave” in a casual aside during a cabinet meeting, as the US scrambles to reopen the strait of Hormuz. The US president made the threat after reports of talks between Iran and Oman about jointly charging a toll for ships passing through the crucial waterway, which has been all but closed since the start of the US-Isra

stories.title: Greener pasture of a shepherd’s life lures Chinese workers penned in by ‘996’ jobs
stories.summary: When an Inner Mongolia farmer sought two herders to tend his 3,000 sheep, he was swamped with applicants including graduates, factory labourers and white-collar workers A Chinese farm owner’s recruitment drive for shepherds has ended in success after his job advert seeking people to work on his Inner Mongolia ranch went viral, drawing the attention of city dwellers struggling to find work and highlighting growing strains in China’s labour market. Zuo Xiaoyong posted an advert on Chinese social m

stories.title: ‘Standing up for our children’: parents divided over London teachers’ strikes
stories.summary: Waltham Forest in the east of the capital has seen a wave of industrial action in schools, with more to come The gates to South Grove primary school in Walthamstow were closed to pupils last week. Teachers were on strike as part of a disparate wave of industrial action by members of the National Education Union (NEU) in schools across the borough of Waltham Forest in east London. Continue reading...

stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Scams come in all shapes and sizes (and none of them are nice), and the government is considering creating rules that would force banks, telcos and digital platforms to automatically reimburse victims of smaller scams of up to $3,000. Labor is considering a range of options as part of a scam protection framework. For smaller losses, $3,000 and under, what we’re proposing is that there should be automatic payment

stories.title: Kaja Kallas warns against walking into Russian ‘trap’ as EU ministers meet for talks – Europe live
stories.summary: EU chief diplomat says ‘not much happening’ in talks as she cautions against focus on personalities rather than strategy Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy confirmed plans for a “major defence package” with Sweden to be announced as part of his visit today ( 10:41 ). He said: “We are preparing a major defence package for Ukraine and a strong step regarding Gripen fighter jets, which will definitely make our combat aviation more effective.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Canada to buy Swedish surveillance plane over US models
stories.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney says the Canadian Armed Forces need the plane to detect and deter threats across the Arctic.

stories.title: Germany news: Health care staff protest plan for major cuts
stories.summary: Medical staff in Germany are protesting proposed cuts that ministers say are necessary to stop spiraling costs. Meanwhile, a World War II bomb meant thousands were evacuated from their homes. DW has more.

stories.title: Australia sues US giant 3M over 'forever chemicals' in firefighting foam
stories.summary: The A$2bn case, which centres on contamination at defence sites, is the largest ever brought by the government.

stories.title: US military conducts second strike against Iran within days as Trump fumes over negotiations
stories.summary: According to the US Central Command, its forces struck four Iranian one-way attack drones posing a threat around the Strait of Hormuz and a ground control station in Bandar Abbas 'that was about to launch a fifth drone'.

stories.title: Oil prices jump after US launches new attacks on Iran
stories.summary: The strikes come despite a ceasefire between Tehran and Washington as the two countries hold peace talks.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian strike injures 11 in Odesa daytime attack, children among injured
stories.summary: Russian daytime strike hits the southern port city of Odesa on 27 May, injuring at least 11 including children, and igniting a huge fire that ravages shops and commercial buildings.

stories.title: Iran says it targeted a US airbase after strike near Bandar Abbas airport
stories.summary: It comes after the US said it had carried out a series of "defensive strikes" against Iran.

stories.title: Heavy Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon after large-scale evacuation orders
stories.summary: The Israeli military has ordered Lebanese residents to leave some 17% of the country's territory.

stories.title: Hopes for peace deal fade as US and Iran exchange fire again
stories.summary: US forces targeted Iranian drones near Bandar Abbas as the IRGC claimed it struck back at a US airbase, while Washington sanctioned Tehran's Hormuz toll authority and both sides published contradictory accounts of a possible deal.

stories.title: Uganda shuts border with DR Congo as cases of rare Ebola type surge
stories.summary: The decision by the Ugandan Ebola task force, which goes against World Health Organization guidance, follows a rise in health workers exposed to Ebola by Congolese patients who crossed before the outbreak was declared.

stories.title: Turkish police fire water cannon at protesters after opposition leader ousted – video
stories.summary: Riot police in Turkey have fired a water cannon and teargas to break up a rally called by the ousted opposition leader Özgür Özel days after a court dismissed him from office. Özel called the lunchtime rally in İzmir as Turkey was poised to shut down for the four-day Eid al-Fitr holiday, which begins on Wednesday. On Sunday, riot police had battered their way into the main opposition CHP’s headquarters in the capital, Ankara, firing teargas and beating party members before throwing them out, Öze

stories.title: Patagonia sues drag queen Pattie Gonia for trademark infringement
stories.summary: Outdoor clothing company is suing US environmentalist drag performer for $1 plus legal fees, claiming ‘we wish we didn’t have to do this’ Patagonia has launched a trademark lawsuit against an environmentalist drag queen named Pattie Gonia, who has accused the outdoor clothing company of “trying to erase an activist”. Wyn Wiley, who performs as Pattie Gonia, has accumulated millions of followers online for their environmental activism , raising almost $4m for non-profits so far. Last year they ra

stories.title: ‘This isn’t freedom’: anger, anxiety and tears as Iran’s internet flickers back
stories.summary: After 88 days of near-total blackout, first reactions to the return of partial connectivity were not celebratory After 88 days of near-total internet blackout in Iran, long-delayed messages, images and poems flooded phones and social media feeds at about 5pm on Tuesday, when still-limited connectivity flickered back to life . The first reactions, however, were not celebratory. Many new posts were threaded with scepticism, anxiety and anger. Continue reading...

stories.title: Cigarette butts for free food? How one group is asking people to rethink litter
stories.summary: The WasteBar food truck hopes the eye-catching deal will change people’s attitude to waste in the Netherlands Using cigarette butts to buy buttery Dutch pancakes? That is the deal one food truck is offering at festivals in the Netherlands as a way to get people thinking about litter. Cigarette butts are the most common form of plastic waste in the world, with more than 4.5tn butts produced every year. In the Netherlands the estimated figure is in the hundreds of millions. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘My hospital room is unbearable’: how the heatwave is affecting Britons
stories.summary: As the UK experiences some of its hottest ever May weather, five people share their concerns – and tips for keeping cool The UK is experiencing some of the hottest May weather ever recorded, with temperatures surpassing 35C in parts of England on Tuesday. Campaigners have warned that Britain’s public buildings are dangerously unprepared for rising temperatures , calling for better cooling systems in hospitals, care homes and other spaces used by vulnerable people. Continue reading...

stories.title: Mitigating Mandelson risks would have been impossible, says former MI6 chief
stories.summary: Vetting of former UK ambassador to Washington warned of ties to senior figures in China, Russia and Israel A former head of MI6 has said it would have been “totally impossible” for the Foreign Office to put in place mitigations to manage Peter Mandelson’s associations with senior figures in China, Russia and Israel when he was the UK’s ambassador to the US. On Wednesday, the Guardian revealed some of the concerns that contributed to security officials recommending that Mandelson be denied develo

stories.title: Wild weather unleashes thunderstorms across south-east Australia, triggering flash flooding
stories.summary: Wet weather batters parts of Queensland, NSW and Tasmania amid warnings of heavy falls on Friday Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Wild weather more typical of summer than the last days of autumn has caused chaos in south-eastern Australia, unleashing a string of severe thunderstorms. Heavy rainfall accompanied by 500,000 lightning strikes across Queensland and New South Wales triggered widespread flash flooding, road closures and travel disruptions. Continue reading..

stories.title: BP boardroom turmoil deepens as ousted chair hits back at ‘lies’ over conduct
stories.summary: Albert Manifold disputes reports about his behaviour and says he always tried to set example The boardroom turmoil at BP deepened after its ousted chair, Albert Manifold, claimed allegations about his conduct were “lies”. In a new and lengthy statement, Manifold disputed reports about his conduct, insisting “at no point in my tenure as chairman of BP has anyone raised with me any issue about my conduct or my relationship with my colleagues”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Victorian greyhound trainer avoids ban despite dog being found with cocaine in system
stories.summary: Tribunal decision on Geoffrey Dalton comes as advocacy group releases data showing 30 greyhounds have died at or after Victorian races this year Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A veteran greyhound trainer whose dog was found to have cocaine in its system at a race has avoided a temporary ban despite the industry regulator calling for one. The Victorian Civil and Administrative Tribunal (Vcat) on Thursday decided not to impose tougher penalties on Geoffrey Dalton, who

stories.title: Samsung memory chip staff in line for £310,000 bonuses after AI profit-sharing deal
stories.summary: Agreement averts strike and shows latest impact of AI boom as two more chipmakers join $1tn club Employees at Samsung Electronics’ memory chip division are to receive bonuses averaging about £310,000 each through a landmark profit-sharing agreement, as the AI boom drives up chipmakers’ profits. Fears of a strike at Samsung were averted on Wednesday after two unions for the world’s largest memory chipmaker said 74% of the 62,616 workers who cast their votes had backed the deal. Continue reading..

stories.title: Canada to order military plane fleet from Sweden in shift from US suppliers
stories.summary: Mark Carney announces purchase of Saab’s GlobalEye early warning aircraft to patrol Arctic territory Canada has announced plans to buy a fleet of early warning planes from Sweden’s Saab rather than a competing option from Boeing, as the country seeks to reduce reliance on US defense firms. Mark Carney, the prime minister, said on Wednesday that Canada would opt for Saab’s GlobalEye, which is based on Bombardier’s Global 6500 jet. Boeing’s E-7 Wedgetail plane – which has suffered from delays and

stories.title: Brazil to revive highway project through Amazon amid concerns
stories.summary: Brazil will invest $75 million to pave a highway that cuts through the center of the Amazon rainforest. Environmentalists argue for strong protections to conserve an ecosystem so crucial to curbing climate change.

stories.title: Iran war: US downs Iranian drones, Tehran retaliates
stories.summary: The US shot down Iranian drones and struck a control center in an effort to "maintain the ceasefire." In retaliation, Tehran struck a US military base. DW has the latest.

stories.title: DOJ launches criminal probe into E. Jean Carroll — reports
stories.summary: The US Justice Department has launched a criminal inquiry into former magazine writer E. Jean Carroll, who accused Donald Trump of sexually assaulting her at a department store 30 years ago.

stories.title: Kenya: Deadly fire breaks out at girls' boarding school
stories.summary: At least 16 students were killed and 73 injured at a girls' boarding school in central Kenya, authorities said.

stories.title: Crises in Amhara, Tigray cloud Ethiopia's election 2026
stories.summary: The June 1 elections come as the country debates federalism, governance and national unity in polls that could define the country's democratic future.

stories.title: India news: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah announces resignation
stories.summary: Main headlines in India include DK Shivkumar being set to replace Siddaramaiah as chief minister for the southern state Karnataka, and Muslims celebrating Eid al-Adha across the country. DW has more.

stories.title: Iran war: Oil shortages threaten global energy security
stories.summary: Strategic petroleum reserves are declining rapidly as countries release emergency crude supplies to cushion the economic impact of the Middle East conflict.

stories.title: New Ebola cases in Congo: What you need to know
stories.summary: The virus spreads from direct person-to-person contact. But here's what makes it especially lethal: it persists in corpses, and funeral practices often take place precisely when bodies are most infectious.

stories.title: US: Former CIA agent charged with stealing gold bars from government
stories.summary: A former CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing gold bars worth millions from the US government and stashing them in his home. Investigators also seized foreign currency and luxury watches.

stories.title: Spain: Police raid HQ of ruling PSOE Socialist party in graft probe
stories.summary: Police have searched the headquarters of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's PSOE party investigating possible illegal payments. Sanchez meanwhile said he was still standing by former PM Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero.

stories.title: Turkey: Leadership dispute engulfs opposition CHP
stories.summary: The top figures in Turkey's main opposition party differ on how to resolve the leadership vacuum after a court annulled its 2023 primary. One wants the party delegates to pick a leader, the other wants members to vote.

stories.title: Philippines: New inquiry launched into Duterte-era drug war
stories.summary: A former ICC judge and a Catholic bishop will lead the new EJK Truth Commission looking into the bloody anti-drug campaign under Rodrigo Duterte. The former Philippines' president is set to face trial in November.

stories.title: South Africa: 300 Ghanaians leave amid xenophobic tensions
stories.summary: Hundreds of Ghanaians are leaving South Africa on special repatriation flights as anti-immigration protests and violence against foreigners escalate in the richer African nation.

stories.summary: BBC's Shaimaa Khalil was in the room as Kenneth Iwamansa, who injected Perry with ketamine, was sentenced to 41 months in prison.

stories.title: How my brother went from liberal Hollywood actor to manosphere 'messiah'
stories.summary: The sister of Mexican influencer El Temach tells the BBC she does not recognise him any more.

stories.title: Is 'out of control' US tipping culture spreading overseas?
stories.summary: With US waiting staff getting cross at receiving less than 20%, tips are also on the rise elsewhere.

stories.title: We analysed thousands of Trump's posts - here's what we found
stories.summary: The BBC's Ros Atkins and BBC Verify sifted through thousands of the president's posts.

stories.title: Google worker charged with using internal data to make $1.2m on bets
stories.summary: The longtime Google employee was charged in New York for allegedly breaking insider trading laws.

stories.title: Duterte ICC trial to start on 30 November: What you need to know
stories.summary: Duterte is facing charges relating to a long and bloody war on drugs in which thousands were killed.

stories.title: Australia charges woman who returned from Syria with joining Islamic State
stories.summary: Several groups of women and children who spent years in a Syrian camp have returned in recent months.

stories.title: Jill Biden says she thought husband was having a stroke during 2024 debate
stories.summary: The former US first lady told CBS News that she was frightened by Joe Biden's performance against Donald Trump.

stories.title: Ireland records 30C while Portugal nears 37C in May heat
stories.summary: A record May heatwave sent Irish temperatures above 30C and Portugal’s to 37C, drawing crowds outdoors and renewing worries over climate change.

stories.title: Iranians back online after months-long shutdown but heavy restrictions still remain
stories.summary: Iranian authorities decided to lift some restrictions this week as negotiators appeared to be closing in on a more permanent truce, but many Iranians now fear access could be cut off again, at a moment’s notice.

stories.title: France heat triggers illegal swimming in central Paris
stories.summary: France's exceptional May heat continued on 27 May as temperatures climbed around 33C in Paris. Many residents headed to the Saint-Martin canal to cool off, despite rules prohibiting swimming in the waterway.

stories.title: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.title: Sixteen pupils killed in Kenya school fire
stories.summary: Dozens of people were also injured in the fire, the cause of which is not yet known.

stories.title: UFC fight venue takes shape outside White House for Trump birthday event
stories.summary: Construction has begun on a UFC octagon outside the White House ahead of a fight night linked to Donald Trump’s 80th birthday celebrations and the 250th anniversary of the United States.

stories.title: IRGC warns Iran's coast will become a 'graveyard' if US strikes resume
stories.summary: An IRGC Navy official threatened to turn Iran's entire Gulf coastline into a killing ground if the US resumed strikes, even as negotiators reported the two sides were closer to an initial agreement than at any point since the April ceasefire.

stories.title: Trump-backed challenger beats veteran US senator in Texas primary
stories.summary: Ken Paxton's victory sets up what will be a closely watched contest in November's midterm elections.

stories.title: Hotel that refused to give tourist tap water acted lawfully, Italian court rules
stories.summary: The Italian Supreme Court rejected a tourist's claim that her consumer rights were breached when she was only offered €7 bottled mineral water.

stories.title: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
stories.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.

stories.title: Trump warns Iran to make a deal or 'we'll finish the job'
stories.summary: Speaking at a White House cabinet meeting on Wednesday, the US president said he was unsatisfied with Tehran's offers and in no rush, even as the White House dismissed an Iranian state television report of a draft agreement as a "complete fabrication".

stories.title: Putin offers debt relief to new recruits for Ukraine war
stories.summary: New recruits for Russia's war in Ukraine will have debts up to 10 million rubles canceled under a presidential decree. Another new law reportedly foresees the use of Russian armed forces to protect citizens abroad.

stories.title: Iran's internet flickers back on despite judicial halt, reports claim
stories.summary: Live metrics show partial restoration of connectivity after President Pezeshkian ordered the international internet to be reopened, but an Iranian court has suspended the order before it could fully take effect, leaving Iranians in legal and digital limbo on day 88 of the country's longest blackout.

stories.title: New head of Hamas' military wing killed in strike on Gaza, Israel says
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office described Odeh as one of the "architects" of the October 7 attacks.

stories.title: Thousands in Georgia rally for EU membership as country marks 35th independence anniversary
stories.summary: Waving Georgian and EU flags, thousands of protesters marched through central Tbilisi before staging a rally outside parliament amid a heavy police presence.

stories.title: ‘Mind-bogglingly crazy’: climate experts alarmed by deadly spring heatwaves searing Europe
stories.summary: Scientists warn of ‘new reality’ of heat extremes that claim three times more lives than car crashes and 16 times as many as murderers Malcolm Mistry knew it was going to get “very warm, very quickly” on Monday morning but a slow start out of bed delayed his plans for an early game of cricket with his son. It was already 10am by the time the pair arrived at the sun-soaked nets of their local club in south-west London, and to the embarrassment of the 48-year-old scientist, who played cricket in h

stories.title: Five villagers found alive in Laos cave as search continues for two missing
stories.summary: Video appears to show divers discovering group sitting on a rock surrounded by flood water Rescuers have reached five of seven people who have been trapped for a week in a flooded, remote cave in Laos, after days spent navigating narrow, inundated passageways amid persistent rain. Video footage shared on social media by rescue divers showed the five men crouched together on a rocky ledge in a dark cave chamber, surrounded by muddy water. “There’s no need to cry,” one of the rescue team told the

stories.title: WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warns of ‘catastrophic collision of disease and conflict’, as Uganda closes border with DRC The head of the World Health Organization has called for an immediate ceasefire in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo to help tackle the Ebola outbreak there, as Uganda closed its border with its neighbour in an effort to stop the spread. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus posted on social media that the region was in the midst of a “catastrophic collision of disease and confl

stories.title: New Mandelson revelations cast doubt on claim vetting decision was borderline, Thornberry says – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here Zack Polanski , the Green party leader, posted this on social media about Tony Blair’s latest intervention this morning. Tony Blair. What the billionaire class have paid for. Spot the difference between “Tony Blair says” and “Nigel Farage says” Continue reading...

stories.title: Nearly half a million Russians killed in Ukraine war, UK spy chief says
stories.summary: Anne Keast-Butler says Russian forces are ‘going backwards on the battlefield’ for first time since late 2022 Nearly half a million Russian soldiers have been killed in Ukraine since the start of Vladimir Putin’s invasion more than four years ago, according to a new estimate from the head of the British spy agency GCHQ. Anne Keast-Butler, the chief of the electronic intelligence agency, said in her first speech in the job that Russian forces were “going backwards on the battlefield” inside Ukrai

stories.title: Why is Ferrari facing such a backlash to its first electric car?
stories.summary: The Italian marque has broken with the past with its four-door, €550,000 Luce and traditionalists are furious Ferrari is different from other carmakers, and so are its product launches. So revered is the company in its native Italy that among the first people to sit behind the wheel of its first electric vehicle were the country’s president and the pope. Yet judging by the backlash from investors, some critics and – inevitably – a horde of online commenters, the company may need help from a high

stories.title: US building Ebola quarantine center in Kenya for Americans amid outbreak
stories.summary: Some experts criticize White House approach and say not allowing Americans to return to US hurts treatment efforts The Trump administration is building a quarantine and treatment center in Kenya for Americans affected by the Ebola outbreak, instead of bringing them home. The White House on Wednesday confirmed that the US was setting up a facility in Kenya for Americans to quarantine after Ebola exposure in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany hopes for heat pump boom amid Iran war
stories.summary: The war in the Middle East is threatening fossil fuel supplies and leading fuel and gas prices to rise in Germany. Could that drive a surge in demand for heat pumps?

stories.title: Europe's energy problem isn't green power — it's storage
stories.summary: Solar and wind produce lots of energy — but not always at the right time. More battery storage could help Europe to stabilize prices and replace polluting fossil fuel energy, but roadblocks remain.

stories.title: EU enlargement: Is the bloc ready to welcome new members?
stories.summary: Brussels says the EU must grow, but resistance within the bloc remains strong, with some arguing that it must reform before enlarging. Montenegro remains the only country with a realistic chance of joining soon.

stories.title: Germany: No recovery in sight for the economy
stories.summary: The war in Iran has dashed hopes for economic growth. Germany's pension and healthcare systems are also feeling the strain.

stories.title: Ebola: Uganda closes border with DR Congo as virus spreads
stories.summary: Uganda has shut its border "with immediate effect," despite warnings by the WHO that the move could backfire and cause the disease to spread.

stories.title: Iran war: Trump says US will 'watch over' Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said the vital Strait of Hormuz "must be open to everyone" and "no one can control it." He made a threat to Oman, which is negotiating with Iran to control the vital waterway.

stories.title: Trump urges Middle East states to sign Abraham Accords
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has urged more Middle Eastern countries to join the Abraham Accords that aim to normalize relations with Israel. What does the agreement entail?

stories.title: FIFA World Cup: New York and New Jersey prosecutors probe 'sky-high' ticket prices
stories.summary: US prosecutors have subpoenaed FIFA over expensive World Cup tickets, saying the event is "not an invitation to exploit our residents and visitors."

stories.title: Thousands rally in Tbilisi as Georgia marks Independence Day with pro-EU protests
stories.summary: Protesters in Tbilisi denounce Georgian Dream, accusing it of stalling EU accession, eroding civil liberties and pushing the country towards Russia.

stories.title: In war-torn Gaza, Palestinians mark Eid al-Adha among the rubble
stories.summary: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip marked Eid al-Adha on 27 May under the shadow of war, gathering for prayers in heavily damaged areas of Khan Younis and Gaza City.

stories.title: Greenpeace stages record deep-sea protest against seabed mining
stories.summary: Greenpeace used a robot more than 2,300 metres below the ocean surface to unfurl a “Listen to the Science” banner in protest against deep-sea mining.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia: More than 1.5 million pilgrims perform stoning ritual in Mina
stories.summary: More than 1.5 million Muslim pilgrims gathered in Mina, Saudi Arabia, on 27 May to perform the symbolic stoning ritual that marks a key stage of the annual Hajj pilgrimage.

stories.title: Five of seven people trapped in flooded Laos cave for more than a week found alive, rescuers say
stories.summary: The cave is located in a rugged, remote area in Xaisomboun province’s Longcheng district, about 120 kilometres north of the capital, Vientiane.

stories.title: South Korea detains dissident who fled China in rubber boat
stories.summary: Dong Guangping, an activist and former police officer, spent hours at sea before reaching Korean waters.

stories.title: Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave
stories.summary: The search is continuing for a further two villagers who are still missing, rescuers say.

stories.title: Trump scores a trophy in Texas as he hunts down Republican rivals
stories.summary: The primary defeat of long-time Senator John Cornyn confirms Trump still has a grip on the party, but comes with huge political risks – and Democrats are excited.

stories.title: Egypt marks record year for gas discoveries amid domestic shortage
stories.summary: Egypt’s recent Eni-linked gas finds have lifted hopes of an upstream revival, but analysts say import pressure is unlikely to ease soon.

stories.title: Bodies pulled from rubble near Tyre after deadly Israeli strike in Lebanon
stories.summary: Lebanon rescue workers recovered bodies from the rubble in Burj al-Shamali near the coastal city of Tyre on 27 May, one day after an Israeli strike that Lebanon’s health ministry said killed at least 14 people.

stories.title: Ghana welcomes Pope's apology over Catholic Church's role in slavery
stories.summary: The West African nation was one of the main gateways for the transatlantic slave trade.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV meets Pedro Sánchez at the Vatican ahead of Spain visit
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV received Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez in a private audience at the Apostolic Palace in Vatican City on 27 May, as preparations continue for the pontiff’s planned visit to Spain in June.

stories.title: Trump Board of Peace's official Gaza reconstruction fund is empty, source says
stories.summary: The board is unambiguously led not just by the United States but by Trump personally, who holds the final say and can remain in charge past his presidency.

stories.title: South Africa and Afrikaners reject US claims of humanitarian crisis for white people
stories.summary: Around 6,000 South Africans have moved to the US since the Afrikaner programme started last year, according to the US government.

stories.title: Watch: Moment rescuers find five people trapped in Laos cave
stories.summary: Rescuers in Laos have found five villagers alive inside a flooded cave after they were trapped for a week.

stories.title: Ebola-hit DR Congo faces 'catastrophic collision' of disease and conflict, WHO warns
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said fighting in DR Congo was hampering efforts to stop spread

stories.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for 41 months over actor's ketamine death
stories.summary: Kenneth Iwamasa, who pleaded guilty last year to one count of conspiracy to distribute ketamine causing death, said he "will forever regret" his actions.

stories.title: Egy titkos kormányhatározatra hivatkozva vettek ki legalább százmilliárd forintnyi kiadást az idei költségvetésből
stories.summary: 176 milliárd forint hiányzik az idei költségvetésből csak az autópálya-koncesszió kiadásai közül. Az új kormányon múlik, nyilvánosságra hozzák-e a hiányt okozó döntés hátterét.

stories.title: Gyorsuló ütemben szívja magához a közpénzt Mészárosék autópálya-koncessziója
stories.summary: Nemcsak a NER-es haszonhúzók motiválhatják a kormányt a szerződés felülvizsgálatára vagy megszüntetésére, hanem az is, hogy enélkül irreálisnak tűnik a választási programban megígért útdíjcsökkentés.

stories.title: Két amerikai állam ügyészei is nekimentek a vb-jegyárakkal machináló FIFA-nak
stories.summary: New Jersey és New York ügyésze is adatokat kért be a fociszervezettől.

stories.title: Tényleg tovább nő pár napig a haj és a köröm a halál után? És miért nem?
stories.summary: Miért vannak hullafoltok? Na és hullamerevség? Holtsápadt arc? Megannyi halálosan komoly kérdés.

stories.title: Az amerikai hadsereg légitámadásokat hajtott végre Iránban
stories.summary: Irán közölte, hogy tüzet nyitottak olyan hajókra, amelyek megkíséreltek átkelni a Hormuzi-szoroson, és visszafordulásra kényszerítették őket.

stories.title: Kicsit megbotlik a kánikula, de hétvégén visszatérnek a 30 fokok
stories.summary: Átmeneti jó hír ez a kellemes 25 fok kedvelőinek.

stories.title: Pont azok az ügyek húzódnak a legtovább, amikben a közvélemény azonnali eredményeket követelne
stories.summary: Az elévülés veszélyén kívül semmilyen törvény nem kényszeríti a hatóságokat a gyanúsítottak mihamarabbi megtalálására – ezért is húzódhat el a nyomozás azokban a komplex ügyekben, amik aztán az eleve évi több százezer üggyel terhelt Fővárosi Törvényszékre futnak be. Bíró, ügyész és ügyvédek is válaszoltak arra, miért húzódhatnak el a nyomozások és hogyan lehetne lerövidíteni a bírósági tárgyalásokat.

stories.title: Folyamatosan költöztetik a javítóintézetekben élő gyerekeket az intézmények
stories.summary: A bezárt Szőlő utcai javítóintézet egykori lakóin kívül január óta 70 gyereket helyeztek át másik intézménybe.

stories.title: A Médiahatóság szerint nem jogszerű, hogy a kormány költségvetési biztost küld rájuk
stories.summary: A kormány szerint súlyos aggályok merültek fel az NMHH és a közmédia gazdálkodásánál. A Médiahatóság szerint minden transzparens náluk.

stories.title: Műholdképen a májusi európai hőhullám
stories.summary: Már több halottja van az extrém forróságnak több nyugat-európai országban, sok helyen megdőltek a havi hőmérsékleti rekordok.

stories.title: Gázolt a vonat a mezőberényi állomáson
stories.summary: Késésekre kell számítani.

stories.title: Joe Biden felesége azt hitte, hogy a férje a Trump elleni elnökjelölti vita alatt sztrókot kapott
stories.summary: A 2024 júniusában rendezett vitán a rekedt hangú Biden többször elakadt a beszéde közben.

stories.title: Még hányszor hiszi el az olajpiac Donald Trumpnak, hogy közel a háború vége?
stories.summary: Donald Trump bejelenti, hogy közel a megállapodás Iránnal, erre leesik az olaj ára, majd valamilyen harci cselekmény jelzi, hogy mégsem olyan jó a helyzet, erre felmegy az olaj ára. Hetek óta tart a csiki-csuki.

stories.title: Nyugdíjba megy a Klebelsberg Központ elnöke
stories.summary: A Tisza-kormány közben azt ígéri, egyik első dolguk lesz felülvizsgálni a Klebelsberg Központ működésének rendszerét.

stories.title: Ha leírtad Rogán nevét, a főszerkesztőnek már habzott a szája – indexes dolgozók beszéltek a lap politikai befolyásolásáról
stories.summary: A 444.hu hét olyan munkatárssal interjúzott a választás után, akik az elmúlt években újságíróként vagy szerkesztőként dolgoztak az Indexnél.

stories.title: Bemászott a családi ház ablakán, és az alvó gyerekek mellől lopta el a mobiltelefont
stories.summary: A visszaeső bűnelkövető egy éven át tartotta félelemben a Heves megyei Halmajugra község lakóit, most vádat emeltek ellene.

stories.title: Több mint 3 év börtönre ítélték Matthew Perry asszisztensét, aki beadta neki a ketamint
stories.summary: Kenneth Iwamasa volt az utolsó az öt vádlott közül, akit elítéltek a Jóbarátokból ismert színész 2023-as halálával összefüggésben.

stories.title: Brutálisan megnőtt a belépőszint a hazai lakáspiacon
stories.summary: A legolcsóbb kategóriákban öt év alatt duplázódtak az árak.

stories.title: Lovász László azért hagyta ott a Bochkor Show-t, mert nem értékelték kellőképpen a munkáját
stories.summary: Felmondása óta egyszer sem hallgatta az adást és Bochkor Gáborral sem beszélt.

stories.title: Vizsgálatot indított a MÁV, miután alkalmazottaik hetekig dolgoztak szennyvízben a békéscsabai autóbuszmosóban
stories.summary: A mosó padló-összefolyójában annyi iszap halmozódott fel, hogy nem tudott elfolyni a mosóvíz.

stories.title: Júliustól 873 ezer forinttal csökkenhet az országgyűlési képviselők fizetése – itt a Tisza Párt új javaslata
stories.summary: Drasztikusan megvágják a képviselőknek járó egyéb juttatásokat is. Forsthoffer Ágnes házelnöki tiszteletdíja 1 millió forinttal, az alelnököké 1,2 millióval csökken. A frakciók nem utalhatják át a jövőben működési támogatásukat a párt számlájára, az év végéig megmaradt keretek pedig bekerülnek a központi költségvetésbe.

stories.title: Tarr Zoltán videóján látszik, hogy ledarált iratokkal teli zsákok várták a NAV nyomozóit a kulturális minisztériumban
stories.summary: Tarr szerint gyanús, hogy Hankó Balázs minisztériuma így hagyta ott az épületet.

stories.title: Lefoglalták a hírhedt maffiafőnök kábítószervagyonát
stories.summary: A Cosa Nostra volt vezetője, Messina Denaro szép kis summát, 70 milliárd forintnyi pénzt forgatott be vállalatokba, offshore cégekbe.

stories.title: Hatályon kívül helyezte a másodfokú bíróság a megszűnés szélén álló Szuverenitásvédelmi Hivatal ellen hozott ítéletet
stories.summary: A Fővárosi Ítélőtábla úgy döntött, új eljárást kell lefolytatnia az elsőfokú bíróságnak az ügyben, ami azért indult, mert az Átlátszó szerint Lánczi Tamás hivatala megsértette a lap jó hírnevét.

stories.title: Ukrajna ismét célzottan támadta a tuapszei finomítót, az orosz olajipar büszkeségét
stories.summary: A Fekete-tengeri kikötőváros hatalmas finomítója exportképes termékeket állított elő, de Ukrajna a finomítót és a kikötőt is rendszeresen lövi.

stories.title: Felhőparádéval érkezett Siófokra tegnap a vihar
stories.summary: Nem ufótámadás, nem kísérleti atomrobbantás, egy igazán impozáns zivatarfelhő látható a HungaroMet fotóján.

stories.title: Képernyő helyett élményekre vágynak az európaiak
stories.summary: Az európaiak többsége egyre inkább próbál kiszakadni az online térből, és a nyári hónapokban, ha teheti, valódi élményeket keres. A Mastercard 28 országból 27 ezer embert kérdezett meg: a kutatás rámutatott arra, hogy a válaszadóknak elegük van az algoritmusokból, a folyamatos értesítésekből és az állandó digitális jelenlétből.

stories.title: Nagy Ervin elárulta Tarr Zoltánnak, mit tart a kultúra legégetőbb problémáinak
stories.summary: Az államtitkár azt is elmondta, hogy három helyettes segíti majd a munkáját.

stories.title: Jogerős a Sztárboxban káromkodó Fluor Tomi miatt az RTL-re kiszabott tízmilliós bírság
stories.summary: Fluor Tomi önkívületi állapotban üvöltött a mikrofonba, a médiahatóság azért indított eljárást, mert tizenkettes karikával adták le a műsort.

stories.title: Jogsegélyszolgálatra gyűjtene adományokat a CÖF-CÖKA
stories.summary: Azokat vennék oltalmukba, akiket az elmúlt években végzett tevékenységük folytán jogszerűtlen számonkérésnek vetnek alá.

stories.title: How the plastic bottle cap became a parable for the value of EU regulation | Alberto Alemanno
stories.summary: Supporters of deregulation want Europe to be more like the US. But that would serve only American interests In July 2024, a European Union law came into force requiring plastic bottle caps to remain attached to their bottles. The regulation was widely mocked by social-media jokesters and Silicon Valley billionaires alike. This, people said, was Brussels at its worst: bureaucrats micromanaging, treating citizens like children who couldn’t be trusted to recycle a cap. What went almost entirely unr

stories.title: South Korea overpass collapse kills three in Seoul
stories.summary: South Korea authorities launched an investigation after part of a decades-old overpass collapsed in Seoul on 26 May. The accident killed three people and disrupted rail traffic near the capital’s main station.

stories.title: Turkish police use water cannon ahead of deposed opposition leader's speech in Izmir
stories.summary: The CHP is level with the ruling Justice and Development Party (AKP) in most recent opinion polls and although the next election is not due until 2028, many expect Erdoğan to push for early elections.

stories.title: Oil steadies at $100 and markets stay volatile as US-Iran talks stall
stories.summary: Brent crude trades at $100 per barrel while European indices drop, Asian stocks close mostly flat and US equities open higher. Markets remain volatile as US-Iran talks stall and President Trump reiterates nuclear demands.

stories.title: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei breaks silence to vow no US military bases in the region
stories.summary: In his longest statement while still not appearing in public, Iran's new ayatollah said US military bases would have no place in the region, as CENTCOM acknowledged overnight strikes on missile sites and Iranian boats laying mines, and a tanker was struck near Oman.

stories.title: Starmer urged to intervene in ‘rigged’ Indian prosecution of British human rights activist
stories.summary: Senior lawyers call on prime minister to request Indian prosecutors drop charges that would breach double jeopardy rule Four senior lawyers, including the former attorney general Dominic Grieve, have written to Keir Starmer urging him to request that Indian prosecutors drop charges against the British national Jagtar Singh Johal on the basis that continued prosecution would be in manifest breach of the double jeopardy rule which prevents someone being tried twice for the same offence. Johal has

stories.title: Indians protest over ‘forever chemicals’ after relocation of scandal-hit Italian factory
stories.summary: Lack of Pfas regulations raised in parliament after Guardian revealed former Miteni plant bought by Indian company Protests over the production of cancer-linked Pfas chemicals have spread across India, after an investigation revealed that an Italian factory shut down due to an environmental scandal was bought by an Indian company and partly rebuilt. At the end of last year, the Guardian revealed that the former Miteni plant in Vicenza had been acquired by the Indian company Laxmi Organic Industr

stories.title: Israeli strike on village in eastern Lebanon kills 12 as Israel calls up more troops
stories.summary: The Israeli military did not comment on this particular strike, but said on Monday that it was targeting Hezbollah infrastructure in eastern Lebanon.

stories.title: ‘Makes no sense’: experts doubt pause in US arms sale to Taiwan is due to Iran war
stories.summary: While approval is due soon for $14bn deal, actual deliveries to Taiwan are years away – making ‘Operation Epic Fury’ in the Gulf an unlikely cause The Trump administration’s war against Iran should have no impact on arms sales to Taiwan, experts have said, after a US official suggested a pause in the delivery of a key weapons package was due to the Gulf conflict. Analysts told the Guardian that a $14bn arms package left in limbo after Donald Trump’s meeting with Xi Jinping could take up to six y

stories.title: Dissident detained in South Korea after fleeing China in rubber boat
stories.summary: Dong Guangping has tried to escape on several previous occasions after being jailed for his activism in China A Chinese dissident has washed up on the shores of South Korea after attempting to flee China in a rubber boat. Dong Guangping, 68, is in custody in South Korea, having been detained by the coastguard on Monday evening. He is thought to have travelled more than 30 hours by sea to reach the shores of China’s democratic neighbour. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hivatalosan is felálltak a Tisza által kezdeményezett parlamenti vizsgálóbizottságok
stories.summary: Például kegyelmi ügy, a gyermekvédelem és az MNB-ügy kivizsgálására hozzák létre ezeket.

stories.title: Végigkísérik az életedet ezek a méregdrága rendszerek, amik mögött egyetlen ember áll
stories.summary: A NER-es üzleti világ egy kevésbé reflektorfényben lévő figurája, Fauszt Zoltán cégei több tízmilliárd forint értékben nyertek közbeszerzéseket az elmúlt években. Többek között a Kréta, a Neptun, a jogsiszerzéshez használt szoftver és az állami szférában használt iratkezelő, a Poszeidon is hozzá kötődik. Mi lesz ezekkel az új kormány alatt?

stories.title: Aggódik egy európai hatóság a Mi Hazánk és az AfD pártcsaládjának politikája miatt, a betiltás is szóba került
stories.summary: A Politico által megszerzett hatósági levél szerint megkeresték az EU vezető szerveit, mert az ESN pártcsalád nem tartja be az Unió alapértékeit.

stories.title: Újra dolgozhat a gyerekorvos, aki pénzért kamuzta be a kötelező védőoltások beadását
stories.summary: Olyan súlyos ugyanis az orvoshiány Pest vármegyében.

stories.title: Félmillió orosz veszhetett oda az ukrajnai csatatereken a brit titkosszolgálati vezető szerint
stories.summary: Anne Keast-Butler figyelmeztetett, az oroszok folyamatosan célba veszik Nagy-Britanniát kémkedéssel és hibrid hadviseléssel.

stories.title: Navracsics Tibor megnevezett két fideszes politikust, akikkel szerinte nyerni tudnának 2030-ban
stories.summary: A volt miniszter szerint ehhez az kell, hogy ne legyen árulózás és tiszásozás.

stories.title: Kapuba kotort kipattanóval nyerte a Konferencia Ligát a Crystal Palace
stories.summary: Az egygólos, Rayo Vallecano elleni meccs hozta meg az angol csapat első európai trófeáját.

stories.title: A pedagógusok és diákok terheinek csökkentéséről is egyeztetett Lannert Judit a PDSZ ügyvivőjével
stories.summary: Egyetértettek abban, hogy nagyon sok feszültség, kimondatlan probléma és bizalomvesztés halmozódott fel az oktatásban.

stories.title: Thuróczy Szabolcs képtelen rendesen szívrohamot kapni
stories.summary: Újabb nyár küszöbére értünk, újra itt a Friss Hús rövidfilmes fesztivál az országban. Dragomán-adaptáció, Cannes-t megjárt trópusi rítus, óriási megmenések. Ajánlunk pár filmet a felhozatalból.

stories.title: Ez a regény sokáig kész káosznak tűnik, de higgyék el, van benne rendszer
stories.summary: A Svájcban élő Mihail Siskin műve folyamatos figyelmet és sokszor újraolvasást igényel, ráadásul kifejezetten hosszú idő, mire az olvasó ráhangolódik erre a sajátos logikára. Kritika.

stories.title: Where are US forces stationed in Europe?
stories.summary: The United States operates various bases across the European continent. DW breaks down what these sites are used for, and how many troops are stationed there.

stories.title: Belgium: Train collides with school bus, several dead
stories.summary: A train has crashed into a school bus near a town close to Brussels, killing at least four people, including two children.

stories.title: Ferrari unveils its first all-electric car, the 4-door Luce
stories.summary: The Italian supercar giant has released images of what will be both its first ever pure EV, and its first car that can seat five people. The Luce eyes a younger clientele and the increasingly competitive Chinese market.

stories.title: Ukraine: EU, Germany summon Russian envoys after Moscow tells diplomats to leave Kyiv
stories.summary: The EU and Germany both summoned Russian diplomats to protest attacks on Kyiv and orders for diplomats to leave the city. The UN secretary-general also rebuked Russia in a session on proliferating conflicts worldwide.

stories.title: How did Berlin's Jewish Hospital and hundreds of its patients survive the Holocaust?
stories.summary: When Red Army troops entered Berlin's Jewish Hospital in 1945, they were astonished to discover hundreds of Jews living just a couple of miles away from Hitler's bunker.

stories.title: The German gym fighting the far right with MMA
stories.summary: The far right is increasingly using MMA to recruit new members. But a gym in Chemnitz, in the east of Germany, is fighting back with a martial arts program focused on diversity and empowerment.

stories.title: License to play: The new James Bond video game
stories.summary: James Bond is back, and he's as charismatic and daring as ever. The video game "007 First Light" tells the origin story of the British secret agent — and the stakes are high.

stories.title: First footage released after deadly train and school minibus crash in Belgium
stories.summary: Belgium emergency services were deployed on 26 May after a train collided with a school minibus at a level crossing in Buggenhout, in East Flanders, killing several people and seriously injuring others.

stories.title: US and Armenia pledge to move forward on economic corridor during Rubio visit
stories.summary: Marco Rubio visited Yerevan, backing PM Pashinyan’s pro‑western course and signed TRIPP corridor and strategic partnership deals to boost regional peace.

stories.title: Paris 'punishingly hot' as Western Europe hit by heatwave
stories.summary: Hugh Schofield reports from Paris as a heat dome settled over Western Europe bringing temperatures above the average for May.

stories.title: Watch: UFC arena construction begins at White House ahead of fight
stories.summary: The massive structure will host a cage fight as part of celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

stories.title: One killed and nine missing after chemical explosion at US paper mill
stories.summary: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

stories.title: Israel expands military ground operations in Southern Lebanon as clashes with Hezbollah intensify
stories.summary: Israel’s military said it struck more than 100 Hezbollah sites across southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley area overnight.

stories.title: Ukraine’s drone commander warns Lukashenka about 500 potential targets in Belarus
stories.summary: Amid rising tensions with Minsk, Ukrainian military commander issued a warning to Aliaksandr Lukashenka and warned him against Belarus' deeper involvement n Russia’s all-out war.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV tests Ferrari’s electric future with new Luce supercar
stories.summary: Ferrari presented its first fully electric car, the Ferrari Luce, to Pope Leo XIV during a meeting at Castel Gandolfo on 26 May.

stories.title: Abortion regret is a myth. Irish women don’t need laws to make them ‘reflect’ on their choices | Roe McDermott
stories.summary: More people regret knee surgery than abortion. So why is the patriarchy still scaring us with lifelong torment? Ireland’s parliament, the Dáil, voted down a reproductive rights amendment bill this month that would have abolished the country’s mandatory three-day waiting period for access to an abortion. Supporters of the unsuccessful reform bill, tabled by the Social Democrats, argued that the delay serves no medical purpose. As the bill moved through political debate and media coverage, those d

stories.title: The wind boom Trump couldn't stop
stories.summary: Even with fierce opposition, offshore wind projects are multiplying along US coasts — signaling a massive shift in energy.

stories.title: North Korea fires projectiles including ballistic missile into Yellow Sea, Seoul says
stories.summary: The North has also repeatedly shunned efforts by the South Korean government to improve relations, repeatedly calling Seoul its most "hostile" adversary.

stories.title: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
stories.summary: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate action The World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease. “We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as he urged neighbouring countries to take immediat

stories.title: Germany news: More than 1,900 missing children
stories.summary: Authorities say there are 1,933 missing children cases nationwide, up 6.8% from the year before. And, travellers in Germany seem nervous when it comes to air travel, amid the war in Iran, a survey suggests.

stories.title: Will SpaceX's IPO fund life on Mars — and a trillionaire?
stories.summary: SpaceX is launching history's biggest public listing, propelling Elon Musk's wealth into the stratosphere. DW explores whether the firm's out-of-this-world ambitions are truly cosmic or another black hole for investors.

stories.title: Berlin Hauptbahnhof at 20: A rail hub with symbolic power
stories.summary: Berlin's central station was inaugurated in May 2006. Beyond its functionality, the modern glass-and-steel structure was built to play a key role in reunified Germany.

stories.title: Unseasonal May heat wave grips Europe
stories.summary: The UK and France have registered record May temperatures as western Europe swelters under a "heat dome." Such extremes are occurring more often as the planet warms.
